methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $299M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Electra is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Sandeep Nijhawan and Quoc Pham that has raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc. The peer universe ranges from $120M to $5.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using the $299M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $568M. The peer-median method anchors to the six-company median of $355M across same-sector, same-stage comparables. These two approaches reflect meaningfully different market signals—the stage-progression method suggests upside relative to peer medians, while the peer-median approach reflects observed market pricing for similar-stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $302M (low) to $653M (high), with a median of $462M. The wide dispersion in peer valuations (driven substantially by Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B versus tighter clustering around $300–$500M for others) introduces material uncertainty in the high-end estimate. Key variables affecting placement within this range include revenue traction, technology maturity, and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort.
